title: dryopteris concinna var. elongata
intro: Dryopteris concinna var. elongata, also known as the Elongated Shield Fern, is a type of fern that belongs to the Dryopteris genus, native to East Asia and the Himalayas. Traditionally, the Elongated Shield Fern has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to treat various health conditions such as arthritis and wounds. The plant is believed to have health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and even acting as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. In herbal preparations, the Elongated Shield Fern is typically dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
